 {"id":"bd-3bp.5","title":"Implement JSON renderable (pretty JSON display)","description":"# Implement JSON renderable (pretty JSON display) (optional)

## Objective
Provide an optional renderable that formats and displays JSON in a human-friendly way.

Phase 7 extra; feature-gated.

## Scope
- Pretty-print JSON with indentation.
- Optional coloring/highlighting if syntax highlighting is enabled.
- Graceful handling of invalid JSON (show error + raw text).

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for formatting and error handling.
- Snapshot tests for a few JSON fixtures (small/large/nested).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Valid JSON renders in a stable, pretty format.
- [ ] Invalid JSON renders an explicit error without panicking.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover representative fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:36.616288826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:34:10.165129917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:01:07.129819170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:58.909419880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:59.433016217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"bd-3bp.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bp.6","title":"Implement Layout renderable (advanced grid layout)","description":"# Implement Layout renderable (composition container)

## Objective
Implement a layout composition renderable that can place child renderables using a higher-level layout spec.

This should build on ftui’s layout primitives (Grid/Flex) rather than inventing a parallel layout engine.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/renderables/layout.rs`

## Scope
- A `Layout` container that:
  - accepts children + a layout specification
  - computes placement rects deterministically
  - renders children in a stable order

## Integration Notes
- Prefer delegating placement math to:
  - `bd-2wi` (Grid layout)
  - (optionally) `bd-yzu` (Flex layout)
- Keep this layer as “glue” + ergonomics, not a second constraint solver.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for placement decisions (rect lists) on small examples.
- Snapshot tests for a few composed layouts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layout container can position multiple children deterministically.
- [ ] Placement uses ftui layout primitives (no parallel solver).
- [ ] Unit + snapshot tests exist and are deterministic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:42.211918374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:28:51.700575614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:59.931165042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2wi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:28:43.138559957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:42.211918374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:00.415155711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-3bp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bp.7","title":"Implement Padding renderable (spacing container)","description":"# Implement Padding renderable (spacing container)

## Objective
Implement a padding container that wraps a child renderable and applies `Sides` padding around it.

This is a basic building block for Panel, layouts, and widget composition.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/renderables/padding.rs`

## Scope
- A padding wrapper that:
  - computes an inner rect (frame minus padding)
  - renders the child into the inner rect
  - reports measurement bounds that include padding

## Design Notes
- Padding must never produce negative dimensions; clamp at 0.
- If padding consumes all space, child render is a no-op.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for inner-rect calculation across edge cases:
  - zero padding
  - padding larger than frame
  - asymmetric padding
- Snapshot tests showing padding effect with a simple child.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Padding correctly computes inner rect and clamps at 0.
- [ ] Child rendering is clipped to inner rect.
- [ ] Measurement bounds include padding consistently.
- [ ] Unit tests and at least one snapshot test exist.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:48.822726004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:28:34.257701513Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:28:34.257677778Z","close_reason":"Implemented Padding widget wrapper + clipping + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:14:43.394230844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:00.959334944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:01.499464026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3bp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:48.822726004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-3bp.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3bp.8","title":"Implement Panel renderable (bordered box)","description":"# Implement Panel widget/renderable (bordered box + title)

## Objective
Implement a Panel component that draws a border around content with optional title/subtitle and configurable padding.

This is a harness-essential building block (status panels, tool boxes, input chrome).

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/renderables/panel.rs`

## Scope
- Border styles (via `BorderChars` presets + custom):
  - rounded, square, double, heavy, ascii
- Title placement (at least top-left/top-center/top-right)
- Subtitle support (optional, but plan-friendly)
- Title truncation for narrow panels (must be Unicode-width correct)
- Padding between border and content
- Style customization for border and title
- Safe/ASCII-only mode for maximum compatibility

## Integration Notes
- Must render via `Buffer` operations (no direct terminal output).
- Should reuse:
  - `BorderChars` presets (`bd-1ho.4`)
  - padding helpers (`bd-3bp.7`)
  - drawing ops (`bd-3ky.2`)
  - `Text` type for styled titles (`bd-2uk`)

## Tests Required
- Snapshot tests across:
  - widths (narrow/normal/wide)
  - border presets (including ascii)
  - title alignments
  - unicode titles (emoji/combining) with truncation
- Unit tests for layout math (inner rect calculation).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Panel renders borders correctly with all presets and custom borders.
- [ ] Titles are positioned correctly and truncated safely under narrow widths.
- [ ] Padding is applied correctly and never draws out of bounds.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover representative cases and are deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Support all border styles: Rounded, Square, Double, Heavy, Ascii, None
- [ ] Title placement: top-left, top-center, top-right
- [ ] Subtitle placement: bottom-left, bottom-center, bottom-right
- [ ] Title truncation with ellipsis when panel too narrow
- [ ] Content padding (inner spacing from border)
- [ ] Border style/color customization
- [ ] Expand mode fills available width
- [ ] Fit mode uses minimum necessary width
- [ ] ASCII fallback for non-Unicode terminals

### Rendering Requirements
- [ ] Border characters are single-cell (no emoji)
- [ ] Title renders inline with top border
- [ ] Content properly indented from border
- [ ] Wide characters in title handled correctly
- [ ] Minimum width = border (2) + title (if any)

### Measurement Requirements
- [ ] Minimum = content minimum + border + padding
- [ ] Maximum = content maximum + border + padding
- [ ] Respects max_width constraint

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for border style selection
- [ ] tracing::trace! for title truncation decisions
- [ ] tracing::warn! for content overflow

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Snapshot tests for all border styles
- [ ] Unit tests for title placement variants
- [ ] Property test: output width <= specified width
- [ ] Integration test: nested panels","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:56.154048039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:33:21.380449295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:01.991836820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:03.012459619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:59:56.154048039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bp.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:02.497288949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:27:52.648224176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-3bp.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bp.9","title":"Implement Pretty renderable (pretty printing)","description":"# Implement Pretty renderable (optional)

## Objective
Provide an optional “pretty printer” renderable for structured data.

Phase 7 extra; feature-gated.

## Scope
- Pretty-print common Rust structures (or a generic tree-like structure).
- Deterministic formatting (stable ordering where applicable).

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for formatting.
- Snapshot tests for a few representative structures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pretty formatting is deterministic.
- [ ] Snapshot tests cover representative outputs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:00:07.607762605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:34:10.813119346Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:01:07.777541248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.9","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:03.523962015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bp.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:04.019683541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-3bp.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bt4","title":"Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests","description":"# Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). Comprehensive tests ensure the error boundary works correctly in all scenarios.

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    // Test widget that panics on demand
    struct PanicWidget {
        should_panic: bool,
    }
    
    impl Widget for PanicWidget {
        fn render(&self, _area: Rect, _buf: &mut Buffer, _budget: &RenderBudget) {
            if self.should_panic {
                panic\!("Intentional test panic");
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn healthy_widget_renders_normally() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: false };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_none());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn panic_is_caught() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Should NOT panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_some());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn fallback_renders_on_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Check fallback rendered (red border)
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("TestWidget") || content.contains("[\!]"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn recovery_works() {
        let mut widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // First render: panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        // Fix the widget
        boundary.inner.should_panic = false;
        
        // Try recovery
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover());
        
        // Render again
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Should be healthy now
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn max_recovery_attempts_enforced() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_max_recovery(2);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Initial failure
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Try recovery 3 times (should fail after 2)
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 1
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 2
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.try_recover()); // Should fail - max attempts reached
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn reset_clears_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        boundary.reset();
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn custom_fallback_used() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_fallback(|_err| Box::new(Text::raw("CUSTOM FALLBACK")));
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("CUSTOM FALLBACK"));
    }
}
```

## Integration Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn nested_boundaries_work() {
    // Outer boundary
    let inner_widget = ErrorBoundary::new(
        PanicWidget { should_panic: true },
        "InnerWidget"
    );
    let mut outer_boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(inner_widget, "OuterWidget");
    
    let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10));
    
    // Render - should show inner fallback, not outer
    outer_boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
    
    // Outer should be healthy (inner caught the panic)
    assert\!(\!outer_boundary.is_failed());
}

#[test]
fn terminal_state_preserved_after_panic() {
    // Ensure buffer is in valid state after widget panic
    let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
    let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
    let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10));
    
    // Fill with known pattern
    buf.fill(Cell::new('X'));
    
    boundary.render(Rect::new(5, 5, 10, 3), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
    
    // Area outside boundary should be unchanged
    assert_eq\!(buf.get(0, 0).content, CellContent::from_char('X'));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] 100% coverage of ErrorBoundary public API
- [ ] Tests run without flakiness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:19.532697381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:56.582799419Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:56.582761508Z","close_reason":"15 comprehensive tests already implemented in error_boundary.rs covering healthy rendering, panic catching, failed state, recovery transitions, max attempts, reset, empty/small area, panic payload extraction, and sibling isolation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bt4","depends_on_id":"bd-1nn0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:29.223277443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3c29","title":"Implement macro playback with timing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:49.286118695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:28:37.214154616Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:28:37.214132224Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3c29","depends_on_id":"bd-1ezv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:33.269180855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
